d: The Bond Park Challenge Course is an outdoor learning facility that is more than a recreational experience. Our guests engage in a facilitated experience that focuses on teamwork.  Our Challenge Course, is often called a "ropes course”.

The park consists of over 29 acres of hillside woodland with nearly 2 miles of well defined, steep and strenuous hiking trails. There are several areas with ample clearing, which provides beautiful views of the eastern Butler mountain Range.

Elev. 4266; Elve. Here 4925

The Laurel Knob Overlook was originally called Licklog Ridge Overlook, a name that still appears frequently on Blue Ridge Parkway literature. The forest before you is the first tract of land ever purchased by the U. S. Forest Service back in 1911.
Stone Wall ruins view point
Contained within Rocky Fork State Park, this vista views the water shed of Rocky Fork Creek.
Rock outcrop high above Barnes Creek.

A sprawling, beautiful view of the forest. During winter, when the trees are clear, you can also get a long view of the Watauga Lake. A fence near the signpost has the names of lovers who have visited the point.
View of the North Linville Gorge Valley and a deep chasm between this rock outcropping and Table Rock. Good spot for resting, lots of rocks and shade nearby.

n: Rough Ridge Lookout - Grandfather Mountain, NC.

d: Good view of the dam
End of walking trail, can see the marsh near the river
